prompt: The method is supposed to swap two variables, but after the call x and y are unchanged. What is the bug?
choices: int[] cannot be passed as a method parameter
swap reassigns the local reference parameters a and b, but that does not change x and y in main
temp must be declared as int, not int[]
Arrays passed to methods are always copied
explanation: Reassigning a parameter (a = b) only changes the local copy of the reference. x and y in main still point to their original arrays. To actually swap contents, swap a[0] and b[0] instead: int temp=a[0]; a[0]=b[0]; b[0]=temp;
